title: Bridge Essay: Neurobiological effects of urban built and natural environment on mental health: systematic review.
concept: Nature connectedness — biophilia hypothesis, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ecology/Animism** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nature connectedness — biophilia hypothesis, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience.

**Neurobiological effects of urban built and natural environment on mental health: systematic review.** - Rev Environ Health (2023). DOI: 10.1515/reveh-2021-0137. PMID: 35112526. Although rapid global urbanization improves people in many ways, it also increases the prevalence of major mental disorders in urban communities. Exposure to natural surroundings, whether real or virtual, on the other hand, has been found to reduce arousal and stress. The purpose of this study is to provide an overview of the existing literature on how brain function changes when exposed to natural and urban settings.
